title: atriplex hastata var. patula
intro: Atriplex hastata var. patula, also known as the prairie orache, is a plant species belonging to the amaranth family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. The herbal preparation of Atriplex hastata var. patula typically involves infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, although it can also be used in tincture or salve form for topical applications.
